data.message:
fix: aarch64 vmm

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

data.content: Ah, nothing screams "I’m totally on top of my game" like two identical "fix" commits for what I can only assume is an epic typo in "premission." Also, "bump" as a commit message? Truly a masterclass in ambiguity—did you bump your head or just the version? And remember, "feat" commits are supposed to imply joy and excitement, not just another mediocre version of buddy_alloc.

data.message:
fix: update GetKernelPagePermissions exe premission

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

fix: update GetKernelPagePermissions exe premission

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

feat: new buddy_alloc version

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

data.content: Wow, three commits and yet somehow we managed to write two identical messages about adding SCTLR_EL1 bits. It’s a bold strategy to put "feat" in front of a commit that could’ve been a single line in a README, but hey, who doesn’t love a good bit of repetition? Maybe next week, we can try branching out from the bits and adding some actual features, or at least some variety in our commit messages!
